description: Disease mechanisms across cardiovascular, pulmonary, renal, and hematologic systems.

facts.context: The hemodynamic profile of cardiogenic shock shows:
facts.truth: low CO, high SVR, elevated CVP and elevated PCWP
distractors: high CO, low SVR, elevated CVP and elevated PCWP
low CO, low SVR, decreased CVP and decreased PCWP
normal CO, high SVR, decreased CVP and normal PCWP

facts.context: Distributive shock from sepsis presents with:
facts.truth: low SVR, high or normal CO, and elevated serum lactate
distractors: high SVR, low CO, and markedly decreased serum lactate
normal SVR, normal CO, and undetectable serum lactate
low SVR, absent cardiac output, and normal serum lactate

facts.context: Type I respiratory failure is characterized by:
facts.truth: hypoxemia with normal or low PaCO2 from V/Q mismatch
distractors: hypoxemia with elevated PaCO2 from airway obstruction
normal PaO2 with elevated PaCO2 from pump failure
isolated hypercapnia with preserved oxygenation status

facts.context: Type II respiratory failure is characterized by:
facts.truth: hypoxemia with hypercapnia from ventilatory failure
distractors: isolated hypoxemia with low PaCO2 from shunt
normal PaO2 with isolated metabolic acidosis only
hypercapnia with normal PaO2 from diffusion block

facts.truth: simultaneous consumption of fibrinogen and platelets, elevating D-dimer
distractors: isolated platelet destruction with normal fibrinogen and low D-dimer
selective fibrinogen elevation with preserved platelet count
increased clotting factor production with decreased D-dimer levels

facts.context: RAAS activation in heart failure produces:
facts.truth: angiotensin II causing vasoconstriction and aldosterone-mediated sodium retention
distractors: angiotensin II causing vasodilation and aldosterone-mediated potassium retention
renin directly causing vasodilation independent of angiotensin production
aldosterone suppression with angiotensin-mediated sodium excretion in kidneys

facts.truth: renal vasoconstriction driven by splanchnic vasodilation in cirrhosis
distractors: direct hepatotoxin damage to renal tubular epithelial cells
renal vasodilation from portal hypertension in liver disease
autoimmune attack on glomeruli triggered by hepatic fibrosis

facts.context: Elevated anion gap metabolic acidosis causes include:
facts.truth: methanol, uremia, DKA, propylene glycol, INH, lactate, ethylene glycol, salicylates
distractors: normal saline infusion, diarrhea, renal tubular acidosis, acetazolamide use
hyperchloremia, pancreatic fistula, ureteral diversion, and cholestyramine
aldosterone excess, Cushing syndrome, licorice ingestion, and amphotericin B

facts.context: Pre-renal AKI is identified by laboratory findings of:
facts.truth: BUN/Cr ratio > 20:1, FeNa < 1%, and urine osmolality > 500
distractors: BUN/Cr ratio < 10:1, FeNa > 3%, and urine osmolality < 300
BUN/Cr ratio > 20:1, FeNa > 2%, and urine osmolality < 350
BUN/Cr ratio < 15:1, FeNa < 1%, and urine osmolality < 250

facts.context: Acute tubular necrosis (ATN) is diagnosed by:
facts.truth: muddy brown casts on urinalysis, FeNa > 2%, urine osmolality < 350
distractors: hyaline casts on urinalysis, FeNa < 1%, urine osmolality > 500
red blood cell casts, FeNa > 2%, and urine osmolality > 600
waxy casts on urinalysis, FeNa < 0.5%, urine osmolality = 300

facts.context: Pulmonary embolism causes right heart strain with:
facts.truth: elevated RV pressure and interventricular septal bowing (D-shaped septum)
distractors: decreased RV pressure and leftward interventricular septal shift
bilateral ventricular dilation with normal septal position on echo
isolated left ventricular failure with decreased pulmonary pressures

facts.truth: Beck's triad (hypotension, JVD, muffled sounds) and pulsus paradoxus > 10 mmHg
distractors: Virchow's triad (stasis, injury, hypercoagulability) and pulsus alternans
Beck's triad (hypertension, JVD, loud sounds) and pulsus paradoxus < 5 mmHg
Cushing's triad (hypertension, bradycardia, irregular respirations) plus JVD
